summary: Implemented rule-based anomaly detection in internal/detector/anomaly.go with DetectAnomalies function detecting slow calls (>=30s) and unauthorized file access (paths outside project dir). Also implemented ResolveProjectDir helper using git rev-parse with cwd fallback.
keyDecisions: Used json.Unmarshal to extract file_path from tool Input JSON for robust cross-platform parsing
normalizePath simplified to ignore filepath.Abs error since it only fails on empty strings
Context chain built by appending each tool_use ToolName to a running slice during iteration
Used filepath.Separator in isInsideDir prefix check to prevent false prefix matches (e.g. /projectextra vs /project)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: DetectAnomalies(entries []TurnEntry, projectDir string) []Anomaly implemented in internal/detector/anomaly.go
File path outside projectDir triggers AnomalyUnauthorized
Boundary test: exactly 30.000s marked slow (>=30s inclusive)
Project dir determined by git rev-parse --show-toplevel; fallback to cwd
Paths normalized to absolute before comparison
Each Anomaly includes LineNum, ToolName, Duration, FilePath, Context

notes: ResolveProjectDir at 83.3% coverage (git failure fallback path untested without mocking). Overall package coverage at 95.0% meets requirement. Windows-compatible tests use json.Marshal for proper path escaping in JSON strings.
